This prophecy coming true within three years would confirm the prophet's mission and belief in all his other prophecies. Concerning Moab it is foretold that their chief cities should be surprised by the enemy. Great changes, and very dismal ones, may be made in a very little time. The Moabites will have recourse to their idols for relief. Ungodly men, when in trouble, have no comforter. But they are seldom brought by their terrors to approach our forgiving God with true sorrow and believing prayer. There should be cries of grief through the land. It is poor relief to have many fellow-sufferers, fellow-mourners. The courage of their soldiers will fail. God can easily deprive a nation of that on which it most depends for strength and defense. These calamities should cause grief in the neighboring parts. Though enemies to Israel, yet as our fellow creatures, it should be grievous to see them in such distress. In [reference omitted], the prophet describes the woeful lamentations heard through the country of Moab when it became a prey to the Assyrian army. The country will be plundered. And famine is usually the sad effect of war. Those who are eager to get abundance of this world and to lay up what they have gotten, little consider how soon it may all be taken from them. While we warn our enemies to escape from ruin, let us pray for them that they may seek and find forgiveness of their sins.
